title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Long Walk', a dystopian movie adaptation of a Stephen King novel, is streaming on HBO Max.
'Project Hail Mary', starring Ryan Gosling, is a blockbuster sci-fi film now available on Prime Video.
Netflix is streaming 'Little House on the Prairie', a new adaptation of Laura Ingalls Wilder's classic tale.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela is named to the 2026 MLB All-Star team, replacing the injured Aaron Judge.
Rafaela, a center fielder for the Red Sox, has a .283 average with 8 home runs and 39 RBIs this season.
Rafaela joins three other Red Sox players as All-Stars, contributing to Boston's recent success in MLB.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut will compete in the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship on July 11 at The Brook Casino, Seabrook.
Geoffrey Esper, current chicken wing record holder with 281 wings in 10 minutes, will compete against Chestnut.
The competition involves eating the most weight in wings within 10 minutes using Kowloon's Saugus Wings.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, marking the end of Boston's World Cup 2026 hosting.
Kylian Mbappé scored his eighth goal of the tournament, contributing to France's win despite an ankle knock.
Boston hosted seven matches, with significant attendance, and became a cultural focal point for visiting fans.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola of Gloucester is accused of trying to kill Marianna DiMercurio with a WD-40-soaked rag.
DiMercurio claims Palazzola owed her $160,000 and intended to drive them off a bridge to stage a murder-suicide.
Palazzola faces charges including attempted murder and assault; she's scheduled for arraignment on July 21.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Coco Fernandez, Mobar owner in Waltham, became the barber for France's national soccer team during the World Cup.
Fernandez first met star players like Kylian Mbappé after receiving a WhatsApp request for haircuts at Boston's Four Seasons.
He provides haircuts to French and Moroccan national team members, maintaining relationships with players amid extensive security.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox faced a 17-hour travel delay from Chicago due to multiple plane issues before a series in New York.
The delay was attributed to a broken tug and a cockpit light issue, causing the team to stay overnight in Chicago.
MLB rescheduled the game's start time to 7:50 p.m., allowing Boston to play despite arriving late in New York.

title: Photos: See breathtaking tall ships sail toward Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships will dock in Boston Harbor for the Sail Boston event, starting July 11, 2026.
The event is the final stop of the Sail250 tour, featuring ships arriving from New York.
The Five Sisters Race features ships like Germany’s Gorch Fock and Romania's Mircea sailing towards Boston.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Tight ends edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Hunter Henry is the only returning tight end for the Patriots, having played in all 17 games last season.
Eli Raridon, a 6'7" rookie, is expected to contribute, joining other new faces at the position.
The Patriots' depth at tight end relies heavily on Henry, with limited proven experience behind him.

title: Q&A: Gigi Perez reflects on Berklee, Boston, and a special Fenway week alongside Noah Kahan
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Gigi Perez, known for her hit 'Sailor Song', supports Noah Kahan's sold-out shows at Fenway Park.
Perez briefly attended Berklee College of Music before launching her music career on platforms like TikTok.
Her debut album 'At The Beach, In Every Life' released in 2025, earned her VEVO's 'DSCVR Artist of the Year.'
